human_verification.notes: Validated curriculum-specific child-care boundaries, curriculum and assessment evidence handling, privacy gating, repeat-failure escalation, adjacent specialist routing, and commercialization/deployment completeness.

specialist_owned_evidence: Boundary is explicit to curriculum-support operations, evidence checks, stakeholder communication, blocker tracking, and adjacent-lane routing in child-care and family-services operations.
Authority sources anchor the pack in federal child-care and Head Start curriculum, assessment, family-engagement, and privacy rules plus recognized NAEYC early-childhood practice guidance.

Scenarios validate policy-exception handling, missing prerequisites, privacy-sensitive requests, repeat failures, adjacent-lane routing, tenant adaptation, and industry-specific in-scope curriculum work.
The package prevents generic support behavior by requiring curricula, lesson plans, observations, child-assessment artifacts, family communication records, and tenant authority maps.

review_notes: No unreviewed regressions were identified in the package artifacts.
Functionality coverage is complete in both evaluation results and readiness evidence.

notes: The specialist is validated as a spec pack and does not claim retained-implementation runtime behavior.
Promotion to a retained implementation should happen only after repeated production-like usage confirms stable curriculum and privacy retrieval, low escalation noise, and safe blocker handling.
